briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 1, 2026. Today, we will provide updates focusing on domestic and international climate change observations and carbon neutrality efforts, renewable energy market trends, and the severity of the global climate crisis and the international community's response efforts.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Strengthening Domestic Climate Change Monitoring and Expanding Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: Domestically, efforts to monitor climate change and implement carbon neutrality are actively underway. Jeju Gosan Global Atmosphere Watch Station and the southernmost Greenhouse Gas Monitoring Station are striving for more accurate observations by compiling a 'Climate Change Chronicle' [1, 5, 7], which plays a crucial role in accumulating climate change data. Korea South-East Power (KOEN) successfully launched a carbon-neutral lifestyle practice campaign to commemorate Earth Day [2, 6], and the Korea Environmental Preservation Agency (KEPA) signed a business agreement with private companies and the government to spread a culture of carbon neutrality practices [4, 11]. Furthermore, the Korea Climate & Environment Institute (KCEI) is recruiting trainees for Level 1 Greenhouse Gas Emission Trading Scheme Managers, focusing on fostering specialized personnel [3], while Mercedes-Benz's planting of 8,000 trees in Seoul Forest [8, 13] and Asan City's use of reusable containers at the Admiral Yi Sun-sin Festival [12, 15] are notable examples of concrete carbon neutrality practices by corporations and local governments. The National Institute of Ecology's ecological literature contest [9] and forest camps for youth [14] also contribute to fostering environmental awareness among future generations, further reinforcing the argument [10] that carbon neutrality should begin at the local government level.

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Market Changes
sections.content: In the renewable energy sector, while expansion efforts continue both domestically and internationally, signs of market restructuring are also being detected. The Ministry of Climate is discussing a power supervision system in preparation for the '100GW Renewable Energy Era' [16], and Gyeonggi Province has shown positive results by exceeding its target with the deployment of 28.5MW of public sector solar power, achieving 'RE100' [17]. India is accelerating the global transition by setting a 500GW target and continuously expanding renewable energy investments [19], and domestically, the active promotion of a citizen-participatory renewable energy fund has been called for [20]. However, with the impending announcement of Brookfield Renewable's shift to performance losses [18], a forecast [21] suggests that a 'weeding out' process in the renewable energy market will begin in earnest, indicating the need for meticulous management for stable growth. Furthermore, reports [38] that LNG industry stakeholders are attempting to obstruct international discussions on shipping decarbonization highlight the complex interests involved in the renewable energy transition process.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Global Climate Crisis and Warnings
sections.content: Globally, the severity of the climate crisis is being warned, and its impacts are deepening across our daily lives and society as a whole. Reports [27, 34] indicating a nearly 60% reduction in mountain snow over the past 40 years present direct evidence of climate change, exacerbating regional crises such as deepening water scarcity in Africa [35]. Climate change is increasing the risk of stroke through extreme weather and pollution [24], creating hidden costs for work productivity [36], and extending allergy seasons, affecting even the enjoyment of nature [40]. Furthermore, criticisms that authoritarianism exacerbates the climate crisis [23] and demands to speak out against policies worsening climate change [28], along with issues emerging in unexpected areas such as RV parts planning [25] and the judicial system [37], are having severe ripple effects across society. The news of FEMA employees, who criticized cuts during the Trump administration, being reinstated [39] once again reminds us of the importance of natural disaster preparedness.

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Response Discussions and Shifting Perceptions
sections.content: The international community continues various discussions and efforts to address climate change, but challenges are also emerging in some areas. The situation where the U.S. was not invited to an international conference for climate change discussions [22] illustrates the complexity of international cooperation, and a survey showing decreased climate change concern among young Germans [26] suggests the need for continuous attention to shifts in public perception. Nevertheless, figures like Al Gore [29] are fighting against climate change and speaking of hope, and scientific solutions are actively being sought, with the Barcelona Supercomputing Center (BSC) leading digital twin research for climate change adaptation [30]. The transition to agroecology by women farmers in Ghana [31] and the active role of youth at the UN Climate Change Conference in Türkiye this autumn [32] are positive signs demonstrating proactive participation from the field and future generations. However, an NGO's point [33] that climate change is not the sole cause of agricultural and social problems reminds us of the need for a multifaceted approach.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the importance of climate change monitoring, domestic and international efforts to achieve carbon neutrality and expand renewable energy, and the deepening global climate crisis and the international community's multifaceted responses. Addressing the climate crisis requires continuous scientific analysis, policy efforts, and civil society participation from a long-term perspective. This concludes today's climate briefing.
